profile.name: Mihail Kirkov
profile.title: Full-Stack Developer · React/Next.js · Node.js · PHP · AI Integration
profile.summary: Full-stack developer (React/Next.js · Node.js · PHP/Python) with 2+ years of production experience building ERM systems and real-time web applications with hands-on AI integration. EU citizen, relocating to Eindhoven in September 2026.
profile.pitch: Mihail Kirkov — full-stack developer (React/Next · Node · PHP) with real AI-integration experience. 2+ years production. EU citizen (Bulgarian), relocating to Eindhoven September 2026, available August. The lane is frontend polish + AI integration — not generic junior.
profile.availability: Available August 2026 · open to on-site/hybrid (Eindhoven) and remote-EU
profile.eligibility_note: EU citizen (Bulgarian) — no visa, work permit, or sponsorship required

bullets: Drive feature development across the React/TypeScript and PHP ERM systems while staying hands-on.
Run feature planning, deadline setting, and delivery coordination.
Built and maintained two production ERM systems on Dolibarr (10+ custom PHP modules total).
Primary: an internal logistics ERM for the ~20-person parent company — built a new React/TypeScript UI and a Node.js real-time event server replacing legacy interfaces.
Second: a multi-frontend ERM (3 distinct React apps) for a German client supporting operations across ~50 partner companies.
Additional work in Next.js and Python.
Provided individual support for varying skill levels.

projects.name: Lead-HQ
projects.title: Lead-HQ
projects.description: Lead-management platform built solo with Next.js 15, Supabase, and TanStack Table. Integrates Apify for Google-Maps scraping and a custom email-enrichment pipeline — plus an AI grader that scores every scraped lead automatically. An AI feature shipped end to end, from scrape to scored pipeline.
projects.shortDescription: Lead-management platform with Google-Maps scraping, email enrichment, and an AI grader that scores every scraped lead automatically.
projects.longDescription:
Problem: sales outreach started from raw, unscored lists, so time was wasted qualifying weak prospects by hand.

Approach: built a solo Next.js 15 + Supabase platform that scrapes Google Maps through Apify, runs a custom email-enrichment pipeline, and feeds every lead through an AI grader that scores it automatically — results land in a sortable TanStack-Table pipeline.

Outcome: an AI feature shipped end to end, from raw scrape to a scored, prioritised lead pipeline.

projects.name: Izlei
projects.title: Izlei
projects.description: Anonymous posting web app for a Bulgarian client; user-submitted posts with a custom admin panel for approval/editing/deletion.
projects.shortDescription: Anonymous posting app for a Bulgarian client, with a custom moderation admin panel.
projects.longDescription:
Problem: the client needed a space for anonymous user submissions without exposing identities, while keeping full editorial control.

Approach: built with Next.js, TypeScript, and Prisma over PostgreSQL — public anonymous posting backed by a custom admin panel for approving, editing, and deleting posts.

Outcome: a moderated anonymous board the client runs end to end themselves.

projects.name: Ignight
projects.title: Ignight
projects.description: Real-time online multiplayer games platform. React/TypeScript frontend + Node.js/Express backend, WebSockets for live game state, Docker for deployment.
projects.shortDescription: Real-time multiplayer games platform with live game state synced over WebSockets.
projects.longDescription:
Problem: deliver multiplayer games that stay perfectly in sync across every connected player in real time.

Approach: a React/TypeScript frontend with a Node.js/Express backend, WebSockets driving live game state, all containerised with Docker for deployment.

Outcome: a working real-time multiplayer platform running live game sessions.
